Description: Indexing refers to a search engine reading and storing a site's pages so they can appear in search results. Not Present means Google cannot find or display the page at all.
Score Explanation: 754 pages are currently indexed. An additional 5,600 pages are not indexed. Of these, 700 are flagged as duplicate pages without a user-selected canonical URL, and approximately 200 have been crawled by Google but not indexed — likely also duplicates. There has been a significant increase from 1,600 to 5,600 not-indexed pages over the past three months, which suggests a recent site change or plugin is generating duplicate URLs.
How to correct?: The site has a large volume of duplicate pages that Google is choosing not to index. To fix this: first, identify the source of the duplicate URLs — a security plugin is generating parameterised URLs (e.g. ?wordfence_lp=1) that Google is crawling as separate pages. Add a Disallow rule to the robots.txt file for these URL patterns to prevent Googlebot from wasting crawl budget on them. Second, review all pages flagged as duplicates without a user-selected canonical URL and add a canonical tag pointing to the correct version of each page. Third, once duplicates are resolved, resubmit the sitemap in Search Console to prompt a re-crawl.
Comments: Indexation issues identified: (1) Duplicated pages without user-selected canonicals; (2) 404 errors addressed in the Redirect Plan; (3) Alternative pages with proper canonical tags addressed in the Canonical Tag Issues report; (4) Parameterised URLs generated by security plugin.

Description: An SSL certificate encrypts the connection between the site and visitors. Without a valid SSL certificate the browser shows a security warning and visitors are likely to leave the site immediately.
Score Explanation: All URLs are served over HTTPS and no security warnings are present in the browser. The SSL certificate is valid and covers all subdomains in use.
How to correct?: No action required. Continue monitoring the SSL expiry date and ensure auto-renewal is enabled to prevent any future lapse.

Description: The sitemap is a file that tells search engines which pages exist on the site, when they were last updated, and how to navigate the site structure.
Score Explanation: The sitemap is present at domain.com/sitemap.xml and has been submitted to Search Console. However, three issues were identified: (1) The recipes section is listed in the sitemap but cannot be navigated to from the homepage, meaning users and search engines cannot discover it through normal browsing. (2) A generic logo image is used as the image attribute for 78% of product entries in the sitemap instead of the actual product image. (3) Several URLs in the sitemap return 404 errors — these are old pages that have been removed but not cleaned from the sitemap.
How to correct?: The sitemap requires three updates. First, either add navigation to the recipes section from the homepage or remove it from the sitemap until it is properly accessible. Second, update the image attribute for each product URL in the sitemap to point to the correct product image rather than the generic logo. Third, remove all 404 URLs from the sitemap — a full list of these URLs is available in the Redirect Plan report. After making these changes, resubmit the updated sitemap in Search Console.
Comments: A PDF page linked from the sitemap (domain.com/secretmenu/) is live but not accessible from navigation. URLs with 'bvstate=pg' in the URL — approximately 554 entries — should be disallowed in robots.txt to prevent them appearing in the sitemap at all. A list of all sitemap URLs to add and remove is available in the Sitemap Delta report.
Sources: GSC Coverage Report (gsc-coverage.csv); Sitemap Delta Report
